devanagari: सुदामोपाख्यानम् – पत्‍नीप्रेरणया सुदाम्नो भगवत् समीपे गमनं भगवता तस्य सत्कारश्च । श्रीराजोवाच । भगवन् यानि चान्यानि मुकुन्दस्य महात्मनः...
english_devnagari: sudāmopākhyānam – pat‍nīpreraṇayā sudāmno bhagavat samīpe gamanaṃ bhagavatā tasya satkāraśca śrīrājovāca bhagavan yāni cānyāni mukundasya mahātmanaḥ vīryāṇyanantavīryasya śrotumicchāmi he prabho

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative plural from √ vīr class 10 verb], [vocative plural from √ vīr class 10 verb], [accusative plural from √ vīr class 10 verb]

translation:
King Parīkṣit requested:
O worshipful sage! We desire to hear of other glorious exploits of the noble-souled Kṛṣṇa of infinite powers and glory, O master.

devanagari: को नु श्रुत्वासकृद् ब्रह्मन् उत्तमःश्लोकसत्कथाः । विरमेत विशेषज्ञो विषण्णः काममार्गणैः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: ko nu śrutvāsakṛd brahman uttamaḥślokasatkathāḥ virameta viśeṣajño viṣaṇṇaḥ kāmamārgaṇaiḥ
translation: What person (on earth) who has the power of discernment of what is excellent and the quintessence of things but has suffered sorely from the shafts of passions and desires, and had once the opportunity of listening to the holy and true stories of the Lord of hallowed renown, would desist from listening to them, O Brāhmaṇa sage?

devanagari: सा वाग् यया तस्य गुणान् गृणीते । करौ च तत्कर्मकरौ मनश्च । स्मरेद् वसन्तं स्थिरजङ्गमेषु । श्रृणोति तत्पुण्यकथाः स कर्णः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: sā vāg yayā tasya guṇān gṛṇīte karau ca tatkarmakarau manaśca smared vasantaṃ sthirajaṅgameṣu śrṛṇoti tatpuṇyakathāḥ sa karṇaḥ
translation: That is the only real speech (tongue) with which Lord’s excellences are extolled. Those hands deserve their designation as hands which engage themselves in his service. That mind is worth its name which contemplates on him who abides in all the mobile and immobile creation. Only that ear (is the blessed and) deserves to be called such (viz. the ear) which listens to the holy stories of the Lord.

devanagari: शिरस्तु तस्योभयलिङ्गमानं । एत्तदेव यत्पश्यति तद्धि चक्षुः । अङ्गानि विष्णोरथ तज्जनानां । पादोदकं यानि भजन्ति नित्यम् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: śirastu tasyobhayaliṅgamānaṃ ettadeva yatpaśyati taddhi cakṣuḥ aṅgāni viṣṇoratha tajjanānāṃ pādodakaṃ yāni bhajanti nityam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ liṅg class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ liṅg class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: That head deserves to be tailed such as bows down to him as manifested in both mobile or immobile forms (or lays itself on the ground to bow to him and his votaries). That is the real eye which sees the Lord (in everything and everywhere). Those are the real parts of the body, which always resort to the waters that wash the feet of the Lord as well as those of his devotees (i.e. sprinkle themselves and get purified with those waters).[1]
purport:
Footnote [1]:
VT. cautions that this sprinkling is limited to the limbs of the upper part (nābher ūrdhvam) of the body.

devanagari: सूत उवाच । विष्णुरातेन सम्पृष्टो भगवान् बादरायणिः । वासुदेवे भगवति निमग्नहृदयोऽब्रवीत् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: sūta uvāca viṣṇurātena sampṛṣṭo bhagavān bādarāyaṇiḥ vāsudeve bhagavati nimagnahṛdayo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ su class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ su class 2 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Sūta said:
When the worshipful son of Bādarāyaṇa was thus requested by king Parīkṣit, Śuka whose heart is immersed in (the meditations of) the glorious Lord Vāsudeva, said.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । कृष्णस्यासीत् सखा कश्चिद् ब्राह्मणो ब्रह्मवित्तमः । विरक्त इन्द्रियार्थेषु प्रशान्तात्मा जितेन्द्रियः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca kṛṣṇasyāsīt sakhā kaścid brāhmaṇo brahmavittamaḥ virakta indriyārtheṣu praśāntātmā jitendriyaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[aorist active third single], [injunctive active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka began to narrate:
There was a certain Brāhmaṇa friend of Lord Kṛṣṇa. He was a pastmaster in the knowledge of the Vedas (or the supreme Brahman), free from attachment to the objects of senses. He was perfectly serene in mind and possessed complete control over his senses.

devanagari: यदृच्छयोपपन्नेन वर्तमानो गृहाश्रमी । तस्य भार्या कुचैलस्य क्षुत्क्षामा च तथाविधा ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: yadṛcchayopapannena vartamāno gṛhāśramī tasya bhāryā kucailasya kṣutkṣāmā ca tathāvidhā
translation: Though he was leading the life of a householder, he subsisted on whatever came his way without asking or trying for it. He was (naturally) ill-clad and his wife also who was like him (had no sufficient clothing to cover her person) but was of the same spirit, had grown emaciated with hunger.

devanagari: पतिव्रता पतिं प्राह म्लायता वदनेन सा । दरिद्रं सीदमाना वै वेपमानाभिगम्य च ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: pativratā patiṃ prāha mlāyatā vadanena sā daridraṃ sīdamānā vai vepamānābhigamya ca

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: That poverty-stricken chaste lady who was devoted to her husband but whose body was trembling (with weakness) and with a withered countenance, approached her husband and submitted:

devanagari: ननु ब्रह्मन् भगवतः सखा साक्षाच्छ्रियः पतिः । ब्रह्मण्यश्च शरण्यश्च भगवान् सात्वतर्षभः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: nanu brahman bhagavataḥ sakhā sākṣācchriyaḥ patiḥ brahmaṇyaśca śaraṇyaśca bhagavān sātvatarṣabhaḥ
translation: “Is it not a fact, O Brāhmaṇa, that Kṛṣṇa, the Consort of Lakṣmī incarnate, is a personal friend of your worshipful self? The glorious Lord, the leader of Sātvatas, is a friend and devotee of Brāhmaṇas and a worthy asylum to those who seek shelter in him.

devanagari: तमुपैहि महाभाग साधूनां च परायणम् । दास्यति द्रविणं भूरि सीदते ते कुटुम्बिने ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: tamupaihi mahābhāga sādhūnāṃ ca parāyaṇam dāsyati draviṇaṃ bhūri sīdate te kuṭumbine
translation: Blessed Sir, be pleased to approach him who is the ultimate resort of the righteous and saintly people. He will (immediately understand your circumstances and) bestow abundant wealth on you, who are a poverty-stricken householder.

devanagari: आस्तेऽधुना द्वारवत्यां भोजवृष्ण्यन्धकेश्वरः । स्मरतः पादकमलं आत्मानमपि यच्छति । किं न्वर्थकामान् भजतो नात्यभीष्टान्जगद्गुरुः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: āste smarataḥ pādakamalaṃ ātmānamapi yacchati kiṃ nvarthakāmān bhajato nātyabhīṣṭānjagadguruḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [ablative single from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ smṛ class 1 verb]
[ablative single from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ smṛ class 1 verb]

translation: That Lord of Bhoja, Vṛṣṇi and Andhaka clans now resides at Dvārakā. If his lotus feet are remembered, he gives away even his own self (to them. He is so generous). Will not that father and preceptor of the world confer wealth and worldly desires on his devotees, as both these are not very- important comparatively (as gifts to his votaries).

devanagari: स एवं भार्यया विप्रो बहुशः प्रार्थितो मृदु । अयं हि परमो लाभ उत्तमःश्लोकदर्शनम् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: sa evaṃ bhāryayā vipro bahuśaḥ prārthito mṛdu ayaṃ hi paramo lābha uttamaḥślokadarśanam
translation: When he was thus softly entreated by his wife many times, the soft-natured Brāhmaṇa thought, “It will be a supreme blessing at least that I shall gain at the sight of the Lord of holy renown.

devanagari: इति सञ्चिन्त्य मनसा गमनाय मतिं दधे । अप्यस्त्युपायनं किञ्चिद् गृहे कल्याणि दीयताम् ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: iti sañcintya manasā gamanāya matiṃ dadhe apyastyupāyanaṃ kiñcid gṛhe kalyāṇi dīyatām

translation: With these considerations, he made up his mind to go (to Dvārakā). (As it was customary not to see a king or a guest empty-handed, but to offer him something as a present) he said, “Is there anything in the house worth giving as a present to Kṛṣṇa? If so, give that with me, O blessed lady.”

devanagari: याचित्वा चतुरो मुष्टीन् विप्रान् पृथुकतण्डुलान् । चैलखण्डेन तान् बद्ध्वा भर्त्रे प्रादादुपायनम् ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: yācitvā caturo muṣṭīn viprān pṛthukataṇḍulān cailakhaṇḍena tān baddhvā bhartre prādādupāyanam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ prā class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ prā class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ prā class 2 verb]

translation: The lady solicited the (neighbouring) Brāhmaṇas to give her some pṛthukas (fried and beaten rice). Having secured four handfuls of such rice, she tyed them up in a rag and handed over them to her husband as ‘Present’ to Lord Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: स तानादाय विप्राग्र्यः प्रययौ द्वारकां किल । कृष्णसन्दर्शनं मह्यं कथं स्यादिति चिन्तयन् ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: sa tānādāya viprāgryaḥ prayayau dvārakāṃ kila kṛṣṇasandarśanaṃ mahyaṃ kathaṃ syāditi cintayan

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ mah class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ mah ]
[nominative single from √ mah class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ mah class 10 verb], [nominative single from √ mah ], [accusative single from √ mah ]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: With those handfuls of rice as present, the Brāhmaṇa travelled the city of Dvārakā anxiously thinking all the while, “How will it be possible for me to gain a sight of Lord Kṛṣṇa?”

devanagari: त्रीणि गुल्मान्यतीयाय तिस्रः कक्षाश्च सद्विजः । विप्रोऽगम्यान्धकवृष्णीनां गृहेष्वच्युतधर्मिणाम् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: trīṇi gulmānyatīyāya tisraḥ kakṣāśca sadvijaḥ vipro

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ i class 2 verb], [locative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Accompanied by other Brāhmaṇas, he crossed the three check posts of guards[2] and the defensive ramparts of the city and the mansions of Andhakas and Vṛṣṇis who followed the path of righteousness ordained by Lord Kṛṣṇa—which were inaccessible to other persons.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
According to verse quoted by VT. a gulma or checkpost consists of nine elephants, nine chariots, twentyseven horsemen and fortyfive footsoldiers.

devanagari: गृहं द्व्यष्टसहस्राणां महिषीणां हरेर्द्विजः । विवेशैकतमं श्रीमद् ब्रह्मानन्दं गतो यथा ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: gṛhaṃ dvyaṣṭasahasrāṇāṃ mahiṣīṇāṃ harerdvijaḥ viveśaikatamaṃ śrīmad brahmānandaṃ gato yathā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Out of the sixteen thousand palaces of the queens of Kṛṣṇa, the Brāhmaṇa entered one of them which appeared specially affluent and splendid, with the feeling of supreme bliss in merging with the Brahman.

devanagari: तं विलोक्याच्युतो दूरात् प्रियापर्यङ्कमास्थितः । सहसोत्थाय चाभ्येत्य दोर्भ्यां पर्यग्रहीन्मुदा ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ vilokyācyuto dūrāt priyāparyaṅkamāsthitaḥ sahasotthāya cābhyetya dorbhyāṃ paryagrahīnmudā
translation: Noticing from a distance that the Brāhmaṇa was coming, Lord Kṛṣṇa who then occupied a sofa with his beloved (Rukmiṇī), instantly rose from his seat, went forward to receive him and joyfully embraced him closely with both of his arms.

devanagari: सख्युः प्रियस्य विप्रर्षेः अङ्गसङ्गातिनिर्वृतः । प्रीतो व्यमुञ्चदब्बिन्दून् नेत्राभ्यां पुष्करेक्षणः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: sakhyuḥ priyasya viprarṣeḥ aṅgasaṅgātinirvṛtaḥ prīto vyamuñcadabbindūn netrābhyāṃ puṣkarekṣaṇaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ prī class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ prī class 9 verb]

translation: The lotus-eyed Lord felt extremely happy at the touch of the person of his beloved friend, the Brāhmaṇa sage and he shed tears of joy from his eyes.

devanagari: अथोपवेश्य पर्यङ्के स्वयं सख्युः समर्हणम् । उपहृत्यावनिज्यास्य पादौ पादावनेजनीः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: athopaveśya paryaṅke svayaṃ sakhyuḥ samarhaṇam upahṛtyāvanijyāsya pādau pādāvanejanīḥ
translation: Then he seated him on his own couch; and personally washed his feet and sprinkled his own head with the drops of water with which he washed the Brāhmaṇa’s feet. O king Parīkṣit, the Lord who purifies the whole universe, smeared the Brāhmaṇa with excellent perfumes such as pastes of sandal aloe-wood and saffron.

devanagari: अग्रहीच्छिरसा राजन् भगवान् लोकपावनः । व्यलिम्पद् दिव्यगन्धेन चन्दनागुरुकुङ्कमैः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: agrahīcchirasā rājan bhagavān lokapāvanaḥ vyalimpad divyagandhena candanāgurukuṅkamaiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: Then he seated him on his own couch; and personally washed his feet and sprinkled his own head with the drops of water with which he washed the Brāhmaṇa’s feet. O king Parīkṣit, the Lord who purifies the whole universe, smeared the Brāhmaṇa with excellent perfumes such as pastes of sandal aloe-wood and saffron.

devanagari: धूपैः सुरभिभिर्मित्रं प्रदीपावलिभिर्मुदा । अर्चित्वाऽवेद्य ताम्बूलं गां च स्वागतमब्रवीत् ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: dhūpaiḥ surabhibhirmitraṃ pradīpāvalibhirmudā arcitvā
translation: Having worshipped his friend with perfumed fumes and rows of lamps, he offered him a cow as a gift, a betel-leaf (with its usual contents of areca-nut powder, cloves, etc.) and greeted him warmly with sweet words of welcome.

devanagari: कुचैलं मलिनं क्षामं द्विजं धमनिसंततम् । देवी पर्यचरत् साक्षात् चामरव्यजनेन वै ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: kucailaṃ malinaṃ kṣāmaṃ dvijaṃ dhamanisaṃtatam devī paryacarat sākṣāt cāmaravyajanena vai

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: It is said that the queen Rukmiṇī herself, with a chowrie in her hand, fanned the ill-clad Brāhmaṇa of emaciated dusty body the veins of which were visible.

devanagari: अन्तःपुरजनो दृष्ट्वा कृष्णेनामलकीर्तिना । विस्मितोऽभूदतिप्रीत्या अवधूतं सभाजितम् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: antaḥpurajano dṛṣṭvā kṛṣṇenāmalakīrtinā vismito

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ sabhāj class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ sabhāj class 10 verb]

translation: The ladies in the harem were really astonished to see that a semi-nude Brāhmaṇa is worshipped with so much affection by Lord Kṛṣṇa of such a spotless reputation.

devanagari: किमनेन कृतं पुण्यं अवधूतेन भिक्षुणा । श्रिया हीनेन लोकेऽस्मिन् गर्हितेनाधमेन च ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: kimanena kṛtaṃ puṇyaṃ avadhūtena bhikṣuṇā śriyā hīnena loke

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

[nominative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]
[nominative single from √ puṇ class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ puṇ class 10 verb]
[instrumental single from √ hā class 1 verb], [instrumental single from √ hā class 3 verb]
[instrumental single from √ hā class 1 verb], [instrumental single from √ hā class 3 verb]

translation: (They commented): “What meritorious act must have been performed by this naked beggarly Brāhmaṇa, destitute of wealth and censured as worthless in the world, that he was greatly honoured by the preceptor of the three worlds, the abode of the goddess Śrī. But he discarded the goddess Lakṣmī who was occupying the sofa with him and embraced him as if he were his elder brother.”

devanagari: योऽसौ त्रिलोकगुरुणा श्रीनिवासेन सम्भृतः । पर्यङ्कस्थां श्रियं हित्वा परिष्वक्तोऽग्रजो यथा ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: yo paryaṅkasthāṃ śriyaṃ hitvā pariṣvakto
translation: (They commented): “What meritorious act must have been performed by this naked beggarly Brāhmaṇa, destitute of wealth and censured as worthless in the world, that he was greatly honoured by the preceptor of the three worlds, the abode of the goddess Śrī. But he discarded the goddess Lakṣmī who was occupying the sofa with him and embraced him as if he were his elder brother.”

devanagari: कथयां चक्रतुर्गाथाः पूर्वा गुरुकुले सतोः । आत्मनोर्ललिता राजन् करौ गृह्य परस्परम् ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: kathayāṃ cakraturgāthāḥ pūrvā gurukule satoḥ ātmanorlalitā rājan karau gṛhya parasparam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ lal class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ lal class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ lal ], [vocative plural from √ lal ]

[nominative plural from √ lal class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ lal class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ lal class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ lal ], [vocative plural from √ lal ], [accusative plural from √ lal ]
[nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: O king! Holding each other by the hand, they recounted to each other the sweet stories of the incidents that happened while both of them lived together in their teacher’s hermitage in their student days.

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । अपि ब्रह्मन्गुरुकुलाद् भवता लब्धदक्षिणात् । समावृत्तेन धर्मज्ञ भार्योढा सदृशी न वा ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvāca api brahmangurukulād bhavatā labdhadakṣiṇāt samāvṛttena dharmajña bhāryoḍhā sadṛśī na vā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
The glorious Lord enquired:
O dear Brāhmaṇa, expert in religion! Please tell me if, after the completion of your course of education and after honouring your teacher with the payment of fee for education, you have married a wife suitable to you or have not done so as yet.

devanagari: प्रायो गृहेषु ते चित्तं अकामविहितं तथा । नैवातिप्रीयसे विद्वन् धनेषु विदितं हि मे ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: prāyo gṛheṣu te cittaṃ akāmavihitaṃ tathā naivātiprīyase vidvan dhaneṣu viditaṃ hi me

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle second single], [present passive second single]
[nominative single from √ vid class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ vid class 2 verb]

translation: I learn that even as a householder, your mind is not interested in objects of enjoyment and being a learned man you are not so much after money.

devanagari: केचित् कुर्वन्ति कर्माणि कामैरहतचेतसः । त्यजन्तः प्रकृतीर्दैवीः यथाहं लोकसङ्ग्रहम् ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: kecit kurvanti karmāṇi kāmairahatacetasaḥ tyajantaḥ prakṛtīrdaivīḥ yathāhaṃ lokasaṅgraham

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb]
[imperfect active second plural], [imperfect middle third single]
[nominative plural from √ tyaj class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ tyaj class 1 verb]

translation: Like unto myself who perform action to set example to the world, there are some people who discharge their duties without being affected by desires at heart and avoid the fascinations created by the celestial potency of the Lord.

devanagari: कच्चिद् गुरुकुले वासं ब्रह्मन् स्मरसि नौ यतः । द्विजो विज्ञाय विज्ञेयं तमसः पारमश्नुते ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: kaccid gurukule vāsaṃ brahman smarasi nau yataḥ dvijo vijñāya vijñeyaṃ tamasaḥ pāramaśnute

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]
[ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Do you remember, O Brāhmaṇa. our stay in the house of our preceptor? It is there that a twice-born one knows everything worth knowing from his preceptor and ultimately reaches beyond the darkness (of saṃsāra).

devanagari: स वै सत्कर्मणां साक्षाद् द्विजातेरिह सम्भवः । आद्योऽङ्ग यत्राश्रमिणां यथाहं ज्ञानदो गुरुः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: sa vai satkarmaṇāṃ sākṣād dvijāteriha sambhavaḥ ādyo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: Dear friend! He from whom one is born (and thus invested with the body) is the first guru (preceptor). He from whom one becomes twice-born (by performance of the upanayana—investiture of the sacred thread) and becomes eligible for study of Vedas and performance of religious duties is the second preceptor. And one who imparts spiritual knowledge directly to persons belonging to all āśramas (stages of life)—is the Third guru (preceptor) and is veritably identical with me.

devanagari: नन्वर्थकोविदा ब्रह्मन् वर्णाश्रमवतामिह । ये मया गुरुणा वाचा तरन्त्यञ्जो भवार्णवम् ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: nanvarthakovidā brahman varṇāśramavatāmiha ye mayā guruṇā vācā tarantyañjo bhavārṇavam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ tṝ class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ tṝ class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ tṝ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ tṝ class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ tṝ class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ tṝ class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ tṝ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ tṝ class 1 verb]

translation: (Hence, those who resort to me as the preceptor giving enlightenment in spiritual knowledge, and cross the saṃsāra are the really intelligent ones) O Brāhmaṇa! Those who, in this mundane existence, obtain oral instructions (in spiritual matters) from me as their preceptor, easily cross the ocean of saṃsāra, as they know the real essence in the order of varṇas (social classes) and āśramas (stages of life).

devanagari: नाहमिज्याप्रजातिभ्यां तपसोपशमेन वा । तुष्येयं सर्वभूतात्मा गुरुशुश्रूषया यथा ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: nāhamijyāprajātibhyāṃ tapasopaśamena vā tuṣyeyaṃ sarvabhūtātmā guruśuśrūṣayā yathā
translation: I abide in all beings as their Inner Controller. Just as I am pleased by rendering services (to the spiritual preceptor), I shall not be pleased (that much) by performance of sacrifices (or the duties of a householder’s life), by birth in excellent family, by the performance of the investiture of the sacred thread of upanayana (implying the observance of duties of a brahmacārin or a celibate person). Nor shall I be pleased so much with penance (in the Vānaprastha stage of life) or by the path of quietism (followed by sannyāsins).

devanagari: अपि नः स्मर्यते ब्रह्मन् वृत्तं निवसतां गुरौ । गुरुदारैश्चोदितानां इन्धनानयने क्वचित् ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: api naḥ smaryate brahman vṛttaṃ nivasatāṃ gurau gurudāraiścoditānāṃ indhanānayane kvacit

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vṛt class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ vṛt class 1 verb]

translation: Do you remember, O Brāhmaṇa, the incident which happened while we were staying at our preceptor’s hermitage and were once sent by our preceptor’s wife to bring fuel.

devanagari: प्रविष्टानां महारण्यं अपर्तौ सुमहद् द्विज । वातवर्षं अभूत्तीव्रं निष्ठुराः स्तनयित्‍नवः ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: praviṣṭānāṃ mahāraṇyaṃ apartau sumahad dvija vātavarṣaṃ abhūttīvraṃ niṣṭhurāḥ stanayit‍navaḥ
translation: And how when we entered into the thick jungle, suddenly, out of season, there was an outbreak of a mighty violent storm, heavy down-pour of rain and terrible roaring of thunder over our heads.

devanagari: सूर्यश्चास्तं गतस्तावत् तमसा चावृता दिशः । निम्नं कूलं जलमयं न प्राज्ञायत किञ्चन ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: sūryaścāstaṃ gatastāvat tamasā cāvṛtā diśaḥ nimnaṃ kūlaṃ jalamayaṃ na prājñāyata kiñcana

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ sūr class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ sūr class 10 verb]

translation: In the meanwhile the sun set and all the quarters were enveloped in darkness and the undulations of the ground being covered under water we could not discern the high or the low level of the land.

devanagari: वयं भृशम्तत्र महानिलाम्बुभिः । निहन्यमाना महुरम्बुसम्प्लवे । दिशोऽविदन्तोऽथ परस्परं वने । गृहीतहस्ताः परिबभ्रिमातुराः ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: vayaṃ bhṛśamtatra mahānilāmbubhiḥ nihanyamānā mahurambusamplave diśo gṛhītahastāḥ paribabhrimāturāḥ
translation: Then failing to discern the direction to be followed and sorely beaten by the repeated violent blasts of wind and showers in the forest flooded with water, we were afraid and holding each other by hand, wandered here and there.

devanagari: एतद् विदित्वा उदिते रवौ सान्दीपनिर्गुरुः । अन्वेषमाणो नः शिष्यान् आचार्योऽपश्यदातुरान् ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: etad viditvā udite ravau sāndīpanirguruḥ anveṣamāṇo naḥ śiṣyān ācāryo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ vad class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ vad class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ vad class 1 verb], [locative single from √ vad class 1 verb]
[nominative dual from √ vad class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ vad class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ vad class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ vad class 1 verb]

translation: Having come to know of this after sunrise, our teacher Sāndīpani came in search of us with his disciples and found us in a bewildered conḍition.

devanagari: अहो हे पुत्रका यूयं अस्मदर्थेऽतिदुःखिताः । आत्मा वै प्राणिनां प्रेष्ठः तं अनादृत्य मत्पराः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: aho he putrakā yūyaṃ asmadarthe ātmā vai prāṇināṃ preṣṭhaḥ taṃ anādṛtya matparāḥ

translation: He cried out, “O my children, you have undergone extreme hardships for our sake. Life or the sou) is verily dearest to all. But you evinced devotion unto me disregarding even that (your life).

devanagari: एतदेव हि सच्छिष्यैः कर्तव्यं गुरुनिष्कृतम् । यद् वै विशुद्धभावेन सर्वार्थात्मार्पणं गुरौ ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: etadeva hi sacchiṣyaiḥ kartavyaṃ guruniṣkṛtam yad vai viśuddhabhāvena sarvārthātmārpaṇaṃ gurau

translation: It is in this manner that a good disciple should repay his debt (of gratitude) to his preceptor, namely that he should offer with a sincere heart, whatever he has with him (including his very life).

devanagari: तुष्टोऽहं भो द्विजश्रेष्ठाः सत्याः सन्तु मनोरथाः । छन्दांस्ययातयामानि भवन्त्विह परत्र च ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: tuṣṭo chandāṃsyayātayāmāni bhavantviha paratra ca
translation: O prominent among twice-borns! I am really very much pleased. Let all your desires be fulfilled. May your knowledge of the Vedas never fail you either here or hereafter.”

devanagari: इत्थंविधान्यनेकानि वसतां गुरुवेश्मसु । गुरोरनुग्रहेणैव पुमान् पूर्णः प्रशान्तये ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: itthaṃvidhānyanekāni vasatāṃ guruveśmasu guroranugraheṇaiva pumān pūrṇaḥ praśāntaye

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ pṝ class 3 verb], [nominative single from √ pṝ class 6 verb], [nominative single from √ pṝ class 9 verb]

translation: Many other such incidents took place during our residence in the house of our preceptor. It is by the grace of the preceptor that a man becomes perfect and eligible for supreme peace.

devanagari: श्रीब्राह्मण उवाच । किमस्माभिरनिर्वृत्तं देवदेव जगद्गुरो । भवता सत्यकामेन येषां वासो गुरावभूत् ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībrāhmaṇa uvāca kimasmābhiranirvṛttaṃ devadeva jagadguro bhavatā satyakāmena yeṣāṃ vāso gurāvabhūt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
The Brāhmaṇa (Śrīdāman) replied:
O God of gods! O Preceptor of the universe! When we stayed in the preceptor’s house, what was there that we did not accomplish through the help of you whose resolutions always turn out to be true.

devanagari: यस्य च्छन्दोमयं ब्रह्म देह आवपनं विभो । श्रेयसां तस्य गुरुषु वासोऽत्यन्तविडम्बनम् ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: yasya cchandomayaṃ brahma deha āvapanaṃ vibho śreyasāṃ tasya guruṣu vāso
translation:
O Almighty Lord! Your person is constituted of the Vedas and you are the repository of all blessings; your stay in the house of the preceptor is certainly a wonderful imitation of the ways of the world.
[back to top]

devanagari: इति श्रीमद्‍भागवते महापुराणे पारमहंस्यां । संहितायां दशमस्कन्धे उत्तरार्धे । श्रीदामचरिते अशीतितमोऽध्यायः ॥ ८० ॥
english_devnagari: iti śrīmad‍bhāgavate mahāpurāṇe pāramahaṃsyāṃ saṃhitāyāṃ daśamaskandhe uttarārdhe śrīdāmacarite aśītitamo
